title: Myriopteris chipinquensis
taxonomy: Myriopteris chipinquensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Myriopteris, which is a group of small to medium-sized ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns. Myriopteris chipinquensis is native to Mexico and is a relatively rare species. It is often found in humid, shaded areas and is an epiphyte or lithophyte, growing on rocks or trees.

intro: Myriopteris chipinquensis, also known as Chipinque fern or Resurrection fern, is a species of fern endemic to the Yucatán Peninsula in Mexico, belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Myriopteris chipinquensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments, which are used to harness its potential therapeutic benefits.
